key_insight: HotRelax predicts relaxed crystal structures in a single forward pass, removing iterative optimization and the need for DFT force labels.
engineering_relevance: This could substantially speed up high-throughput materials screening by making structure relaxation faster, simpler, and more scalable.
method: Trained on paired unrelaxed and relaxed structures
evidence_signal: The model was evaluated on five diverse datasets spanning bulk crystals, layered materials, and catalysts, and showed lower errors than several state-of-the-art end-to-end relaxation baselines.
watch_item: Performance should still be validated across new chemistries and edge-case structures where training coverage may be limited.
paper_title: High-order tensor neural network for iteration-free structure relaxation
